column2: Bacillus anthracis oedema toxin combined with Bacillus anthracis lethal toxin
column11: Bacillus anthracis lethal toxin or Bacillus anthracis oedema toxin, each on its own, is not a regulated animal toxin. However, due to their synergistic effect when used in combination, Bacillus anthracis lethal toxin and Bacillus anthracis oedema toxin together are regulated animal toxins. If both toxins are being imported together in an animal, animal product or animal by-product, a permit must be obtained from the CFIA. Please visit  https://inspection.canada.ca/en/animal-health/requirements-handling-pathogens for more details.

This biological agent is an aquatic animal pathogen and, as such, the importation and use of this pathogen are regulated by the Canadian Food Inspection Agency (CFIA). Please contact the CFIA at ImportZoopath@inspection.gc.ca to obtain additional information.

column2: Cholera toxin (including both subunits A and B)
column11: Work with Cholera toxin subunit A or subunit B, separately, is not regulated. Cholera toxin is composed of 5 B subunits and 1 A subunit. Although subunit B is responsible for cell surface receptor binding and internalization of Vibrio cholerae, it does not possess the adenylate cyclase activity of subunit A. Consequently, when it exists independently of subunit A, the B subunit is non-toxic. Furthermore, since binding of subunit A with subunits B is required to generate an active complex, subunit A alone is also non-toxic.

column11: In order to prevent further reassortment, it is strongly recommended to avoid working with various strains at the same time, particularly for in vivo models. Separation of such work can be spatial (e.g., different biosafety cabinet [BSC]) or temporal (e.g., scheduling). The transmissibility of this pathogen justifies the use of a BSC, or other primary containment device for activities with open vessels. Centrifugation to be carried out in sealed safety cups or rotors that are to be unloaded using a mechanism that prevents their release. Inward airflow is required for work involving large animals or large scale production. Respirators to be worn where there is a risk of exposure to infectious aerosols. Containment level 2 (CL2) is required for in vitro work. CL2-Ag is required for in vivo work.
